UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the Versare application, storing app-specific user details and preferences. This entity assumes an external authentication system manages core user identity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Unique identifier for the user from the external authentication system. Used to link the internal UserProfile to an external identity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a luxury artisanal fashion product available in the Versare e-commerce store, featuring details like descriptions, pricing, and imagery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, including its design inspiration, craftsmanship, and materials.

currency.description: The currency in which the product is priced (e.g., 'BRL', 'EUR', 'USD').

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to high-quality images of the product. The first URL in the array is typically the primary product image.

material.description: The primary material(s) used in the product (e.g., 'linen', 'silk', 'organic cotton').

category.description: The primary category of the product (e.g., 'Dresses', 'Accessories', 'Outerwear').

availabilityStatus.description: The current availability status of the product (e.g., 'in_stock', 'pre_order', 'sold_out').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was first added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product details were last updated.

PreSaveItem.title: PreSaveItem
PreSaveItem.description: Records a user's expressed interest in a specific product, functioning as a 'pre-save' or early access wishlist item for products not yet fully available or for future purchase intent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PreSaveItem entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PreSaveItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product entity. (Relationship: Product 1:N PreSaveItem)

preSaveDate.description: Timestamp when the user pre-saved this product.

status.description: The current status of the pre-save item (e.g., 'pending', 'notified', 'purchased', 'removed').

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Each document's ID corresponds to the Firebase Auth UID of the user. This path-based ownership ensures that 'request.auth.uid' directly matches '{userId}', providing inherent authorization independence for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Contains the catalog of all artisanal luxury fashion products. This collection is intended for public read access, with restricted write access (e.g., to administrators).

Stores user-specific pre-save items (early access wishlist). The 'userId' from the path is denormalized, matching the 'userId' field within the document, ensuring authorization independence and secure list operations for a user's own pre-save items.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who pre-saved the item, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: preSaveItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific pre-save entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
- For `UserProfile` (`/user_profiles/{userId}`), the document ID (`userId`) is directly derived from `request.auth.uid`. This makes authorization trivial and atomical, as the user's ID is part of the path, eliminating any need for `get()` calls in security rules to determine ownership.
- For `PreSaveItem` (`/users/{userId}/pre_saves/{preSaveItemId}`), the `userId` from the path (`{userId}`) is denormalized and implicitly matches the `userId` field within the `PreSaveItem` document. This ensures that a user can only access their own pre-save items without the rules needing to fetch parent user data or the `PreSaveItem` document itself to check `userId` ownership. The creation of a new `PreSaveItem` can be a single atomic write without complex rule dependencies.

**Support for QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters):**
- **`/products/{productId}`:** This collection is designed for public read access (or admin write access). Queries for products do not require any user-specific authorization logic, allowing all products to be listed efficiently without filtering issues.
- **`/user_profiles/{userId}`:** Access to a user profile is strictly private, tied to the authenticated user's UID. A query to retrieve a user's profile (`/user_profiles/{request.auth.uid}`) is inherently secure because the path itself enforces the `request.auth.uid == userId` condition. The client is only allowed to request their own profile, eliminating the need for server-side filtering.
- **`/users/{userId}/pre_saves/{preSaveItemId}`:** This subcollection is explicitly structured to support QAPs. When a user requests their list of pre-saved items (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/pre_saves`), the Firestore security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `userId` from the path. This allows clients to fetch all pre-save items for the currently authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) without the server needing to filter results. Any attempt to query another user's pre-saves will be blocked by the rules before any data is returned, ensuring that queries are always secure and performant.
